---
title: "Relapse Analysis"
format: html
---

## Relapse Analysis Activity

A lapse back to alcohol use does not begin when you take a drink of alcohol. Often, things that happen before you drink indicate the beginning of a lapse. Identifying your patterns of behavior will help you recognize and interrupt the lapse.

::: callout
### Instructions

-   Think back to the week leading up to your last lapse.

-   Fill in each category with the events, behaviors, and thoughts you experienced.

-   Reflect on your feelings about each event.
:::

| **Category** | **Events** | **Patterns of Behavior** | Feelings About the Events |
|----|----|----|----|
| Subject / Career Events |  |  |  |
| Personal Events |  |  |  |
| Treatment Events |  |  |  |
| Alcohol-Related Behaviors |  |  |  |
| Behavioral Patterns |  |  |  |
| Relapse Thoughts |  |  |  |
| Health Status |  |  |  |

::: callout
### Looking Back and Learning More

It looks like this may not be your first relapse analysis activity. That’s okay! Each time you reflect, you gain new insights into your patterns and strengths.

#### Reflection Questions

**1. What did you notice this time that you didn’t see before?**

**2. Do any patterns keep showing up across different lapses?**

**3. Were there warning signs you recognized earlier this time?**

**4. What strategies did you try to interrupt the lapse before it happened? How well did they work?**

**5. What could you do differently next time to strengthen your recovery?**
:::
